sections.heading: Simple Present — Full Guide
sections.body:
Uses:
1. Habits: "I wake up at 7am."
2. Facts: "Water boils at 100°C."
3. Scheduled events: "The train leaves at 9am."
4. General truths: "The Earth revolves around the Sun."

Third person rules:
• Most verbs: add -s (works, plays, reads)
• Verbs ending in s, sh, ch, x, o: add -es (washes, watches, goes)
• Verbs ending in consonant+y: change y to ies (study → studies)
• Verbs ending in vowel+y: just add -s (play → plays)

Irregular: have → has

Negatives and questions with "be":
• "She is not tired." / "Is she tired?"
• "They are not ready." / "Are they ready?"

sections.heading: Present Continuous — Full Guide
sections.body:
Uses:
1. Happening now: "I am typing this email."
2. Temporary: "She is living in Delhi this year."
3. Future arrangement: "We are meeting tomorrow."
4. Changing situation: "The world is getting warmer."
5. Annoying habit (with "always"): "He is always complaining!"

Spelling -ing rules:
• Silent e drop: make → making, write → writing
• Double consonant: run → running, sit → sitting, begin → beginning
• -ie change: die → dying, lie → lying, tie → tying
• Normal: work → working, play → playing

Stative verbs (NOT used in continuous):
• Knowing: know, believe, understand, think (opinion)
• Senses: see, hear, smell, taste
• Emotions: love, hate, want, need
• Possession: have, own, belong

sections.heading: Common Mistakes
sections.body:
1. "I am agree" → "I agree" (stative verb)
2. "She work hard" → "She works hard" (missing -s)
3. "He doesn't goes" → "He doesn't go" (double negative)
4. "Do she like it?" → "Does she like it?"
5. "I am knowing the answer" → "I know the answer" (stative)
6. "He is have a car" → "He has a car"

Remember: After don't/doesn't/didn't, always use the BASE FORM of the verb.

summary: The two most important tenses to master first. Simple Present for habits and facts, Present Continuous for now. Watch out for stative verbs — they don't take -ing.
